concept.domains: (topic concept) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c concept oceConcept-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
concept.title: SAM : What if SAM does not work

section.title: Description
section.p: This document describes how to check your SAM key in case SAM does not work.

section.title: License expired
section.p: The license for the SDS is valid for one year.
Well before it expires you will get a mail from ILMS with a new license. Activating this license needs to be done with the License Update procedure See: SAM : The 'License Update' Procedure.

Check your mail for the license. If you can’t find it, contact your local ILMS administrator. The local ILMS administrator might ask you to create a license request file. Then you need to execute SAM : The 'Create License Request' procedure.

section.title: License not valid or certified time expired
section.p: The SAM key also uses a time security mechanism. You have to synchronize the time every 60 days (or earlier) with the WIBU time servers.
Use the procedure “Synchronize certified time” to update the certified time in the SAM key (see SAM : The 'Synchronize certified time' procedure.

If you updated the certified time and you still get this message, then there is no license for this product on the SAM key. Contact your local ILMS administrator. The local ILMS administrator might ask you to create a license request file. Then you need to execute SAM : The 'Create License Request' procedure. If you have got the license you have to activate it with SAM : The 'License Update' Procedure.

section.title: Initial check with CodeMeter Control Center
section.p: When you experience problems with the SAM key, first check whether it is recognized by CodeMeter Control Center.

Launch CodeMeter Control Center (via 'start > Programs > CodeMeter > CodeMeter Control Center') or via the icon on the task bar and you should get a screen that contains information about your SAM key.

It should display the serial number, the version and the capacity. If nothing is displayed, unplug the stick from your laptop and plug it in again. If this doesn’t solve the problem execute the extended troubleshooting steps.

section.title: Extended Troubleshooting steps
section.p: To investigate problems with the SAM keys always a so-called “Event log” and a “CmDust.log” is required.
To get these two files execute the following steps:

li.p: Open CodeMeter Control Center while you SAM key is not connected.
If you encounter problems with CodeMeter Control Center, execute the steps that cause the problem.
Go back to the Event tab, select all lines and copy them (via the Right Mouse Click)
Paste the lines in a standard text file (notepad for example), e.g. Events.txt.
Connect the SAM key to your laptop.
A command prompt window will appear and disappear after a few seconds.
An explorer window will appear showing the content of your user directory.
Here you will find a file called “CmDust-Result.log”.

section.p: Create a PR and include the log files.
